scenarios.scenario: Attacker forges user_id=1 cookie to impersonate admin
scenarios.rationale: Cookie is now an itsdangerous signed token; a raw forged integer is rejected once the 7-day grace window closes (SESSION_MIGRATION_CUTOFF=2026-07-11). Residual: during the grace window a raw integer is still honored — a deliberate, time-bounded tradeoff to avoid mass-logout, documented in the plan.

scenarios.scenario: Attacker tampers with a valid signed cookie (bit-flips the id)
scenarios.rationale: URLSafeTimedSerializer.loads raises BadSignature; resolver returns None. Covered by test_tampered_signed_cookie_rejected.
SIGNED_MAX_AGE=30 days enforced via loads(max_age=...); expired tokens raise SignatureExpired -> None.

scenarios.scenario: OAuth callback re-issues a raw (forgeable) cookie, bypassing signing
scenarios.rationale: Both OAuth-callback set_cookie sites now use sign_user_id(); no raw cookie is ever re-issued post-login. (OAuth state-binding/CSRF hardening remains Phase 2 scope.)

scenarios.scenario: Attacker discovers webhook URL and forges Telegram /stop or /start
scenarios.rationale: Webhook now requires X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token verified via hmac.compare_digest before body parse; fail-closed 403. Requires the one-time setWebhook secret_token registration (deploy-day manual step).

5 failed attempts / 15 min per-username locks further attempts without checking the password (no timing oracle). Residual: username-keyed (not IP) — documented deliberate tradeoff; distributed username-spray is not mitigated by this control.

scenarios.scenario: Guessable default admin/admin123 on fresh install
scenarios.rationale: Hardcoded default removed; admin only created from explicit INITIAL_ADMIN_PASSWORD, else no account + warning. Verified no 'admin123' literal remains.

scenarios.scenario: Concurrent /api/order requests exceed daily trade limit (TOCTOU)
scenarios.rationale: order_lock widened to wrap check+place+record_trade atomically; concurrency test confirms exactly max_trades_per_day succeed. Live-broker latency behavior deferred to deploy-day paper round-trip.

scenarios.scenario: New position opened during emergency max-loss square-off
scenarios.rationale: Background-loop race closed (automation_enabled=False set before the exit loop). NOT ruled out: a user manually re-enabling automation via Telegram /start or the toggle API mid-square-off could still race. Accepted known-gap, tracked in phase1-square-off-reentry-guard backlog note.

scenarios.scenario: Encryption-key rotation orphans all credentials (get_cipher auto-regenerates a key)
scenarios.rationale: Mitigated by pre-restart step (grep -c '^ENCRYPTION_KEY=' .env == 1) in deployment order + execute-agent instruction E2; migration takes an automatic pre-migration backup and runs in a single transaction. Live cutover still depends on the operator following E2.

scenarios.scenario: Secrets remain exploitable from git history after untracking
scenarios.rationale: By explicit user decision NO git history rewrite is performed; historical .env/.db blobs remain in history. Mitigated by provider-secret rotation (Item G2, manual) — exposure is neutralized by rotation, not erasure. Documented, accepted.
